name: Missouri (United States)
description: Three equal horizontal stripes — red on top, white in the middle, blue on the bottom. Centred on the white stripe is a blue ring set with twenty-four white five-pointed stars, enclosing the Great Seal of Missouri (two grizzly bears flanking a divided shield, with a crescent and bear, and the U.S. coat of arms).
history: Designed by Marie Elizabeth Watkins Oliver, the wife of state senator Robert Burett Oliver, working from 1908 onwards; adopted by the Missouri General Assembly and signed by Governor Elliott W. Major on 22 March 1913. The horizontal tricolour echoes the French flag — recalling that Missouri was once part of French Louisiana — while also matching the colours of the United States flag.

features.symbolism: the colours of the United States flag; red for valour, white for purity, blue for vigilance, permanency and justice

features.role: blue ring at the centre of the white stripe encircling the state seal
twenty-four white stars set in the blue ring around the seal
features.symbolism: Missouri's admission as the 24th state of the Union

features.role: Great Seal of Missouri inside the ring
features.symbolism: depicts two grizzly bears supporting a shield divided per pale: dexter, the United States arms; sinister, a crescent moon and a grizzly bear; below the shield, the motto 'SALUS POPULI SUPREMA LEX ESTO'
